ELPAC Level 4
Lexile ≥1400L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Green String Testimony

STS-MS7-L4-1400P-MSSCI7-CFB-NAV-01 • MS Science Grade 7 • Cycling of Matter and Flow of Energy in Ecosystems
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
On nature shows, the camera pulls one animal aside to tell its own side of the story. In this class each student takes a turn speaking as one living thing from a North Woods forest and confessing what it eats and what eats it. Josie has her turn now, and the green arrows on the board keep score, because every arrow shows energy moving from one living thing to the next.
If the fox disappeared from this forest, which arrow would break first, and what would happen to the rabbits?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Wyatt doing?
Look at the picture. Justify what Wyatt is doing with visible evidence, then extend it: explain how his work would help the class model this ecosystem. Use precise academic language.
2
What is Josie holding?
Look at the picture. Justify what Josie is holding with visible evidence, then extend it: explain where a living thing like the one she names would sit in this ecosystem. Use precise academic language.
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Justify what the teacher is doing with visible evidence, then extend it: explain why she chose that image for this lesson. Use precise academic language.
4
What place is this? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Justify what kind of room this is with visible evidence, then extend it: name two features of this room that a different room would not have. Use precise academic language.
5
Tell me about the food web board.
Look at the picture. Justify what the big board shows with visible evidence, then extend it: explain what the green marks between the images tell you about how energy moves. Use precise academic language.
6
What is Harper wearing?
Look at the picture. Justify what Harper is wearing with visible evidence, then extend it: explain what her clothing and the objects she carries suggest about the class activity. Use precise academic language.
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: NGSS MS-LS2-3 — Cycling of Matter and Flow of Energy in Ecosystems. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
